_readme: Single gate for which Quran translation editions the app will EVER offer
as an additional-language download. Built by reviewing
sources/Quran-api/editions.json (a local clone of fawazahmed0/quran-api,
Unlicense) for entries whose author/institution is well-established as
mainstream Ahlus Sunnah wal-Jama'ah — official state Islamic-affairs
bodies, the Saudi King Fahd Quran Printing Complex, IslamHouse's own
quranenc.com translation portal, or long-standing tanzil.net-hosted
translations with no history of sectarian controversy. English is not
listed here — the app already bundles Saheeh International
(eng_ummmuhammad upstream) as its default, per DATA_SOURCES.md.
Every entry's 'link' is the exact fawazahmed0/quran-api jsDelivr CDN URL
tool/build_translation_pack.py downloads from. 'sha256' is filled in by
that script the first time it actually builds a pack for the edition —
a real hash of the validated (6,236-ayah, 114-surah) output file, not a
placeholder — and becomes the permanent pin the app's
TranslationPackService verifies downloads against.
NON-NEGOTIABLE: an edition not listed here is invisible to the app,
full stop. Adding one requires updating this file (with a real
justification) AND re-running the build script — never a runtime
toggle.
Scope note: this is a conservative starter set (one edition per major
world language), not exhaustive. A scholar/maintainer review before
public release is still recommended — this file documents the
reasoning so that review has something concrete to check, not a claim
that the review has already happened.

editions.author: Muhammad Hamidullah
editions.license: Public domain (Unlicense) container repo; translation text distributed freely for Islamic da'wah per tanzil.net's standard terms
editions.justification: The long-standing standard French Quran translation distributed via tanzil.net and printed by the King Fahd Quran Printing Complex; no sectarian controversy.

editions.author: A. S. F. Bubenheim and N. Elyas
editions.license: Public domain (Unlicense) container repo; translation text distributed freely for Islamic da'wah per tanzil.net's standard terms
editions.justification: The most widely distributed German Quran translation, printed by the King Fahd Quran Printing Complex; the de-facto standard mainstream choice.

editions.author: Fateh Muhammad Jalandhry
editions.license: Public domain (Unlicense) container repo; translation text distributed freely for Islamic da'wah per tanzil.net's standard terms
editions.justification: A classic, widely used mainstream Urdu translation with no sectarian affiliation; one of tanzil.net's standard Urdu options.

editions.author: Diyanet Isleri (Turkish Presidency of Religious Affairs)
editions.license: Public domain (Unlicense) container repo; official state-institution translation
editions.justification: Official translation of Turkey's state Sunni religious-affairs authority — a conservative, institutionally vetted choice.

editions.author: King Fahd Quran Printing Complex
editions.license: Public domain (Unlicense) container repo; distributed by quranenc.com (IslamHouse's translation portal)
editions.justification: Directly published by the Saudi King Fahd Quran Printing Complex via quranenc.com — as institutionally Salafi/Ahlus-Sunnah-aligned as a source gets.

editions.author: Islamic Foundation (Montada Edition)
editions.license: Public domain (Unlicense) container repo; distributed by quranenc.com (IslamHouse's translation portal)
editions.justification: The Montada Islamic Foundation edition distributed via quranenc.com (IslamHouse) — same vetted-institution basis as the Indonesian entry.

editions.author: Abu Bakr Zakaria
editions.license: Public domain (Unlicense) container repo; distributed by the Saudi King Fahd Quran Printing Complex's official domain
editions.justification: Sourced directly from qurancomplex.gov.sa (the Saudi King Fahd Quran Printing Complex's own domain) — an institutionally Salafi-aligned source.

editions.author: Elmir Kuliev
editions.license: Public domain (Unlicense) container repo; translation text distributed freely for Islamic da'wah per tanzil.net's standard terms
editions.justification: The most widely regarded and distributed Russian Quran translation, with no sectarian controversy; a tanzil.net standard option.

editions.license: Public domain (Unlicense) container repo; the official Malaysian government (JAKIM)-endorsed translation
editions.justification: The official, government-endorsed (JAKIM) Malay Quran translation — the only Malay edition in the source dataset and a conservative institutional choice.

editions.justification: King Fahd Glorious Quran Printing Complex (qurancomplex.gov.sa) — the Saudi state Quran-printing authority; squarely within this allowlist's vetting bar.
